result: Multi-field per domain is already in use; DOF-extension reduces sudden cut/interface-switch induced residual jumps. Remaining Newton stall (step ~7) likely needs stronger convection/interface stabilization or penalty scaling rather than doubling the fluid unknowns.

action_or_command: Add optional Peclet number diagnostics in examples/turek_fsi_fully_eulerian.py (PYCUTFEM_PECLET_TRACE=1). Prints Pe mean and p-norm (proxy for max) for full fluid domain (Ω+) and cut-region (Ω+∩cut) once per time step and on Newton failure.
result: Lets us confirm whether problematic steps correlate with local Pe≫1 (indicating convection-dominated instability) and tune CIP/SUPG/skew-convection accordingly.

action_or_command: Add optional per-Newton iteration Pe estimate (PYCUTFEM_PECLET_TRACE_NEWTON=1) using DOF-wise |u|∞ scaling (full + cut nodes) so we can see when Newton iterates blow up the velocity field (even if Pe at iteration 0 is small).
result: Helps distinguish “physics is convection dominated” vs “Newton step drives u to huge values,” and correlates line-search failures with Pe spikes during the iteration.

action_or_command: Implement optional pressure stabilization terms on the fluid ghost-edge set in examples/turek_fsi_fully_eulerian.py: jump form h^3[∇p]·[∇q] and average form h^3{∇p·∇q}, controlled by PYCUTFEM_PRESSURE_STAB_JUMP / PYCUTFEM_PRESSURE_STAB_AVG; included in penalty retry scaling and FD term splitting.
result: Aims to damp spurious pressure modes/huge pressure increments during early Newton steps near the moving interface, without requiring pressure pinning.

action_or_command: Fix examples/turek_fsi_fully_eulerian.py: refresh_hansbo_kappa previously re-clipped θ with hard-coded theta_min=1e-3 after each level-set update; now uses the global θ floor (theta_min=max(SLIVER_THETAMIN,1e-12)) so sliver weights/κ are consistent across time steps.
result: Before fix, step 2+ logs showed min θ+=1e-3 regardless of true cut ratio; after fix min θ can drop to 1e-6 with larger w (e.g. max w≈3e2).

action_or_command: Use PYCUTFEM_INTERFACE_RESIDUAL_TRACE=1 on restart (t0=0.02) to decompose interface residual: R_int_pen dominates (|R|_∞≈5.23e8) while flux parts are O(1e4). Residual breakdown at worst DOF also shows interface kernel contribution O(1e9).
result: Indicates Newton stall is driven by a huge velocity jump across Γ (Nitsche penalty term), not by pressure oscillations; focus should be on extension/guess/penalty scaling rather than Taylor–Hood alone.

action_or_command: Add optional stabilization to the kinematic constraint (d_t d - u = 0): SOLID_KINEMATIC_STAB and SOLID_KINEMATIC_STAB_EXP add -α h^s ⟨∇u_s,∇v_d⟩ on Ω_s to damp high-frequency/extension artifacts in u_s.
result: With SOLID_KINEMATIC_STAB=1 and LS_MODE=dealii at step 5 restart, residual decreases much more than baseline but still needs additional robustness tuning to converge at dt=0.005.
